title: Anti-dilution
shortDefinition: A provision protecting investors from ownership reduction if future funding occurs at lower valuations.

content.definition: Anti-dilution protection is a clause in investor contracts that adjusts the price at which an investor's preferred shares convert to common shares if the company raises a subsequent round at a lower valuation. Instead of seeing their ownership percentage shrink, investors get additional shares or a reduced conversion price to protect their stake.
content.whyItMatters: This is a double-edged sword. For investors, it's insurance against down-rounds. For founders and employees, it can be devastating because it often means the anti-dilution adjustment benefits investors by creating new shares, diluting existing shareholders even more. A down-round becomes twice as punishing with aggressive anti-dilution language. This is why you need to fight for the weakest possible anti-dilution provisions.
content.howToApply: Negotiate for weighted-average anti-dilution at worst; full-ratchet is death for founders. With weighted-average, the adjustment is calculated using both the old and new prices, softening the blow. Many founders accept full-ratchet without realizing they're giving up meaningful equity percentage. Model a realistic down-round scenario (say, 30% lower valuation) and calculate how many new shares the anti-dilution clause creates. If it dilutes you by 5%+ on its own, it's too aggressive and should be renegotiated.
commonMistakes: Accepting full-ratchet anti-dilution without understanding the math of future down-round dilution
Not negotiating anti-dilution at all, assuming down-rounds won't happen (they will)
Focusing on current valuation without modeling how anti-dilution punishes down-rounds
content.ideafuelConnection: IdeaFuel's Business Plan Generator models anti-dilution clauses in your cap table projections, showing the exact equity impact if valuations decline. See your real ownership percentage through multiple funding rounds before signing term sheets.

seoDescription: Anti-dilution protects investors from ownership loss in down-rounds. Learn the types and how they affect founder equity.
